subject: Mathematics
text: Solve for x: x² − 5x + 6 = 0
explanation: Factorise: (x−2)(x−3)=0 → x=2 or x=3

subject: Mathematics
text: What is the discriminant of 2x² + 3x − 5 = 0?

subject: Mathematics
text: The sum of roots of x² − 7x + 10 = 0 is:
explanation: Sum of roots = −b/a = −(−7)/1 = 7

subject: Mathematics
text: Which of the following is a perfect square trinomial?

subject: Mathematics
text: Find the product of roots of 3x² − 12x + 9 = 0
explanation: Product = c/a = 9/3 = 3

subject: Mathematics
text: How many real roots does x² + 4 = 0 have?
explanation: Discriminant = −16 < 0. No real roots.

subject: Mathematics
text: Complete the square: x² + 8x = ___ is (x+4)² − ___

subject: Mathematics
text: The vertex of y = x² − 4x + 7 is at:

subject: Mathematics
text: If one root of x² − kx + 12 = 0 is 3, find k.

subject: Mathematics
text: Solve: 2x² = 8. What are the values of x?

subject: Mathematics
text: Evaluate log₂(64)

subject: Mathematics
text: Simplify: log 100 + log 10

subject: Mathematics
text: What is log₃(1/9)?

subject: Mathematics
text: log(a × b) is equal to:
explanation: Product rule: log(ab) = log a + log b

subject: Mathematics
text: If log x = 2, what is x?
explanation: log₁₀ x = 2 → x = 10² = 100

subject: Mathematics
text: Simplify log₂(32)

subject: Mathematics
text: log(a/b) equals:
explanation: Quotient rule: log(a/b) = log a − log b

subject: Mathematics
text: What is the natural log of e?
explanation: ln(e) = 1 by definition

subject: Mathematics
text: If log₅(x) = 3, find x

subject: Mathematics
text: log 1 = ?
explanation: Any base: log(1) = 0 because b⁰ = 1

subject: Mathematics
text: Convert 1101₂ to base 10

subject: Mathematics
text: What is 25₁₀ in binary?

subject: Mathematics
text: Convert 77₈ to base 10

subject: Mathematics
text: What is FF₁₆ in decimal?

subject: Mathematics
text: Convert 11111111₂ to decimal

subject: Mathematics
text: What is 10₁₀ in base 2?

subject: Mathematics
text: Subtract 1010₂ − 0011₂

subject: Mathematics
text: Convert 100₁₀ to octal (base 8)

subject: Mathematics
text: Multiply 11₂ × 11₂

subject: Mathematics
text: Differentiate y = x³
explanation: d/dx(xⁿ) = nxⁿ⁻¹; d/dx(x³) = 3x²

subject: Mathematics
text: Find dy/dx if y = 5x² + 3x
explanation: Differentiating term by term: 10x + 3

subject: Mathematics
text: Differentiate y = sin(x)
explanation: d/dx sin(x) = cos(x)

subject: Mathematics
text: The derivative of a constant is:
explanation: d/dx(c) = 0 for any constant c

subject: Mathematics
text: Differentiate y = eˣ
explanation: eˣ is its own derivative

subject: Mathematics
text: Find the gradient of y = 2x² at x = 3

subject: Mathematics
text: Differentiate y = ln(x)
explanation: d/dx ln(x) = 1/x

subject: Mathematics
text: If f(x) = x⁴ − 2x, find f'(x)
explanation: Differentiate: 4x³ − 2

subject: Mathematics
text: Differentiate y = cos(x)
explanation: d/dx cos(x) = −sin(x)

subject: Mathematics
text: A stationary point occurs when dy/dx =
explanation: Stationary points are where the gradient equals zero

subject: Mathematics
text: Integrate ∫ 2x dx
explanation: ∫2x dx = x² + C

subject: Mathematics
text: Evaluate ∫ x³ dx
explanation: ∫xⁿ dx = xⁿ⁺¹/(n+1) + C

subject: Mathematics
text: ∫ cos(x) dx =
explanation: The integral of cos(x) is sin(x) + C

subject: Mathematics
text: Evaluate ∫₀² 2x dx

subject: Mathematics
text: ∫ eˣ dx =
explanation: ∫eˣ dx = eˣ + C

subject: Mathematics
text: ∫ sin(x) dx =
explanation: ∫sin(x) dx = −cos(x) + C

subject: Mathematics
text: ∫ 1/x dx =
explanation: ∫1/x dx = ln|x| + C

subject: Mathematics
text: What does the constant C represent in integration?
explanation: C is the constant of integration — any constant that disappears on differentiation

subject: Mathematics
text: ∫ 5 dx =
explanation: ∫k dx = kx + C

subject: Mathematics
text: Integration is the reverse of:
explanation: Integration (antidifferentiation) reverses differentiation

subject: Physics
text: A body at 20 m/s decelerates at 4 m/s². How long before it stops?

subject: Physics
text: Displacement when initial velocity is u, acceleration a, time t:
explanation: Second equation of motion: s = ut + ½at²

subject: Physics
text: The unit of acceleration is:
explanation: Acceleration = change in velocity / time

subject: Physics
text: Newton's First Law states an object at rest will:
options: Stay at rest unless acted on by a force
explanation: Law of Inertia: objects maintain state unless acted upon

subject: Physics
text: A car travels 100m in 10s. Its average speed is:
explanation: Speed = distance/time = 100/10 = 10 m/s

subject: Physics
text: Which is a vector quantity?
explanation: Velocity has both magnitude and direction — it is a vector

subject: Physics
text: Using v² = u² + 2as, find v if u=0, a=10, s=5

subject: Physics
text: A body falls freely from rest. After 3s its velocity is: (g=10)

subject: Physics
text: Momentum = ?
explanation: p = mv (momentum = mass × velocity)

subject: Physics
text: The area under a velocity-time graph represents:
explanation: Area under v-t graph = displacement

subject: Physics
text: Ohm's Law states V = ?
explanation: V = IR (Voltage = Current × Resistance)

subject: Physics
text: Unit of resistance is:
explanation: Resistance is measured in Ohms (Ω)

subject: Physics
text: Power P = ?
explanation: P = IV = I²R = V²/R

subject: Physics
text: In series circuits, total resistance is:
explanation: R_total = R1 + R2 + ... in series

subject: Physics
text: The unit of electric charge is:
explanation: Charge is measured in Coulombs (C)

subject: Physics
text: Current I = Q/t means:
explanation: Current = charge / time

subject: Physics
text: In a parallel circuit, the voltage across each component is:
explanation: Voltage is the same across all parallel branches

subject: Physics
text: A 4Ω and 6Ω resistor in parallel give total resistance:

subject: Physics
text: Energy E = ?
explanation: E = Pt (Energy = Power × time)

subject: Physics
text: What does a fuse protect against?
explanation: A fuse melts when current exceeds its rating, breaking the circuit

subject: Physics
text: Wave speed = frequency × ?
explanation: v = fλ (wave speed = frequency × wavelength)

subject: Physics
text: Which wave requires a medium to travel?
explanation: Sound is a mechanical wave — it needs a medium

subject: Physics
text: The number of waves per second is called:
explanation: Frequency = waves per second, unit: Hertz (Hz)

subject: Physics
text: Frequency and period are related by:
explanation: Frequency is the reciprocal of period: f = 1/T

subject: Physics
text: The bending of waves around obstacles is called:
explanation: Diffraction is the bending of waves around obstacles

subject: Physics
text: Speed of light in vacuum is approximately:
explanation: c ≈ 3×10⁸ m/s

subject: Physics
text: A transverse wave vibrates:
explanation: Transverse waves oscillate perpendicular to propagation

subject: Physics
text: Which property of waves determines its loudness for sound?
explanation: Amplitude determines loudness (intensity) of a sound wave

subject: Physics
text: The unit of frequency is:
explanation: Frequency is measured in Hertz (Hz)

subject: Physics
text: Constructive interference occurs when two waves are:
explanation: Constructive interference: crests align — amplitudes add

subject: Physics
text: The law of reflection states the angle of incidence equals:
explanation: Angle of incidence = angle of reflection

subject: Physics
text: A convex lens is also called:
explanation: A convex lens converges parallel rays to a focal point

subject: Physics
text: Total internal reflection occurs when light travels from:
explanation: TIR occurs when going from a denser to less dense medium beyond critical angle

subject: Physics
text: The unit of lens power is:
explanation: Lens power is measured in dioptres: P = 1/f

subject: Physics
text: A concave mirror has its reflecting surface on the:
explanation: Concave mirror curves inward — reflecting surface is on the inside

subject: Physics
text: The bending of light as it passes from one medium to another is:
explanation: Refraction is the bending of light at the interface between two media

subject: Physics
text: A mirage is caused by:
explanation: Mirages result from total internal reflection in layers of hot air

subject: Physics
text: Which colour of visible light has the highest frequency?
explanation: Violet light has the highest frequency in the visible spectrum

subject: Physics
text: Snell's Law: n₁sin θ₁ = ?
explanation: Snell's Law: n₁sin θ₁ = n₂sin θ₂

subject: Physics
text: The eye defect where near objects are seen clearly but far ones are blurred is:
explanation: Myopia (short-sightedness) — near objects clear, far objects blurred

subject: Physics
text: The nucleus of an atom contains:
explanation: The nucleus contains protons (positive) and neutrons (neutral)

subject: Physics
text: The atomic number of an element is the number of:
explanation: Atomic number = number of protons in the nucleus

subject: Physics
text: Alpha particles consist of:
explanation: Alpha particle = helium-4 nucleus: 2p + 2n

subject: Physics
text: Radioactive decay where an electron is emitted is called:
explanation: Beta-minus decay emits an electron from the nucleus

subject: Physics
text: Half-life is the time for ___of a radioactive sample to decay.
explanation: Half-life: time for half the radioactive nuclei to decay

subject: Physics
text: Gamma radiation is best described as:
explanation: Gamma rays are high-frequency electromagnetic radiation

subject: Physics
text: Mass number = ?
explanation: Mass number = number of nucleons (protons + neutrons)

subject: Physics
text: Isotopes are atoms of the same element with different numbers of:
explanation: Isotopes: same atomic number, different mass number (different neutrons)

subject: Physics
text: Which radiation has the greatest penetrating power?
explanation: Gamma radiation penetrates most — needs thick lead or concrete to absorb

subject: Physics
text: Nuclear fission is the splitting of a heavy nucleus into:
explanation: Fission: heavy nucleus splits into two smaller nuclei + energy

subject: English
text: Which literary device gives human qualities to non-human things?
explanation: Personification attributes human characteristics to non-human entities

subject: English
text: The word 'ephemeral' means:
explanation: Ephemeral means lasting for a very short time

subject: English
text: A simile makes a comparison using:
explanation: Similes use 'like' or 'as' to make comparisons

subject: English
text: The main idea of a passage is also called its:
explanation: The central message or main idea of a text is its theme

subject: English
text: Onomatopoeia means words that:
explanation: Onomatopoeia: words that imitate the sound they describe (e.g., 'buzz', 'hiss')

subject: English
text: An antonym is a word that means the:
explanation: Antonyms are words with opposite meanings

subject: English
text: Which figure of speech is 'The world is a stage'?
explanation: A metaphor makes a direct comparison without 'like' or 'as'

subject: English
text: Alliteration is the repetition of:
explanation: Alliteration: repetition of the same initial consonant sound in close words

subject: English
text: A synonym for 'happy' is:
explanation: Synonyms have the same or similar meaning — 'joyful' is a synonym for 'happy'

subject: English
text: The author's attitude toward the subject is called:
explanation: Tone reflects the author's attitude toward the subject matter

subject: English
text: Choose the correct form: He ___ to school every day.
explanation: Third person singular present simple takes 'goes'

subject: English
text: What is the plural of 'datum'?
explanation: The plural of 'datum' is 'data'

subject: English
text: Choose the correct preposition: She is good ___ mathematics.
explanation: Good at + subject/skill

subject: English
text: Identify the adjective: 'The tall boy ran fast.'
explanation: 'Tall' describes the noun 'boy' — it is an adjective

subject: English
text: Which sentence is in the passive voice?
options: The book was read by John
explanation: Passive voice: object + was/were + past participle + by + subject

subject: English
text: The word 'running' in 'Running is fun' functions as a:
explanation: A gerund is a verb ending in -ing used as a noun

subject: English
text: Choose: Neither the boys nor the girl ___ present.
explanation: Neither/nor: verb agrees with the nearest subject ('girl' → 'was')

subject: English
text: What part of speech is 'quickly' in: 'She ran quickly'?
explanation: 'Quickly' modifies the verb 'ran' — it is an adverb

subject: English
text: A word that joins clauses or sentences is a:
explanation: Conjunctions join words, phrases, or clauses

subject: English
text: The suffix '-tion' typically forms a:
explanation: '-tion' converts verbs to nouns (e.g., 'create' → 'creation')

subject: English
text: Which sound is a vowel?
explanation: /æ/ as in 'cat' is a vowel sound; the others are consonants

subject: English
text: Stress in the word 'photograph' falls on:
explanation: PHO-to-graph — stress on first syllable

subject: English
text: How many syllables does 'examination' have?
explanation: ex-am-i-na-tion = 5 syllables

subject: English
text: The /θ/ sound as in 'think' is classified as:
explanation: The 'th' in 'think' is a voiceless dental fricative /θ/

subject: English
text: Which pair are minimal pairs?
explanation: Minimal pairs differ in exactly one phoneme: /ɪ/ vs /ɒ/

subject: English
text: Intonation in a question typically:
explanation: Yes/no questions typically have rising intonation

subject: English
text: The linking sound /w/ appears in:
explanation: go on → /gəʊ wɒn/ — /w/ links the vowel sounds

subject: English
text: How many phonemes are in the word 'ship'?
explanation: /ʃ/ /ɪ/ /p/ — 3 phonemes

subject: English
text: A diphthong is:
options: A vowel glide from one sound to another
explanation: Diphthong: a vowel that changes quality within a single syllable (e.g., /aɪ/ in 'fly')

subject: English
text: Which word has a silent letter?
explanation: 'Kneel' — the 'k' is silent: /niːl/

subject: English
text: When summarising, you should:
explanation: A good summary paraphrases using your own words

subject: English
text: A summary should be:
explanation: A summary condenses the original — it must be shorter

subject: English
text: Which should NOT be included in a summary?
explanation: Personal opinions are NOT included in an objective summary

subject: English
text: The first step in writing a summary is to:
explanation: Understanding the passage fully is the critical first step

subject: English
text: Topic sentence is typically found:
options: At the beginning of a paragraph
explanation: Topic sentences usually open a paragraph and state its main idea

subject: English
text: In WAEC Summary, how many points are usually required?
explanation: WAEC Summary questions typically ask for 5 points in your own words

subject: English
text: Cohesion in writing refers to:
explanation: Cohesion = how well sentences and ideas connect to form unified text

subject: English
text: Which word signals a contrast?
explanation: 'However' signals a contrast or shift in argument

subject: English
text: A précis differs from a summary in that it:
options: Maintains the tone of the original
explanation: A précis preserves the original author's style and tone

subject: English
text: Transition words like 'therefore' indicate:
explanation: 'Therefore' signals a conclusion/result

subject: English
text: An expository essay primarily aims to:
explanation: Expository essays explain, clarify, or inform about a topic

subject: English
text: The introduction of an essay should contain a:
explanation: The thesis statement presents the essay's main argument

subject: English
text: Each body paragraph should have:
explanation: Each paragraph should focus on one main idea for clarity

subject: English
text: A persuasive essay requires you to:
explanation: Persuasive essays present arguments to convince the reader of a position

subject: English
text: The conclusion of an essay should:
explanation: Conclusions restate the thesis and summarise key points

subject: English
text: Which essay type tells a personal story?
explanation: Narrative essays tell a story, often from personal experience

subject: English
text: A descriptive essay uses vivid language to:
explanation: Descriptive essays use sensory details to paint a picture for the reader

subject: English
text: Evidence in an argumentative essay can be:
explanation: Strong arguments use verifiable evidence: statistics, facts, expert examples

subject: English
text: Paragraphing in an essay is important because it:
explanation: Paragraphs organise ideas, making essays clear and readable

subject: English
text: The PEEL structure stands for:
explanation: PEEL: Point (topic sentence), Evidence, Explanation, Link to thesis

subject: Chemistry
text: What is the general formula for alkanes?
explanation: Alkanes are saturated: CₙH₂ₙ₊₂

subject: Chemistry
text: Which functional group defines alcohols?
explanation: Alcohols are characterised by the hydroxyl (−OH) group

subject: Chemistry
text: Alkenes contain at least one:
explanation: Alkenes are unsaturated hydrocarbons with at least one C=C double bond

subject: Chemistry
text: What type of reaction converts an alkene to an alkane?
explanation: Hydrogenation adds H₂ across a double bond to give an alkane

subject: Chemistry
text: Methane is a member of which homologous series?
explanation: Methane (CH₄) is the simplest alkane

subject: Chemistry
text: Fermentation of glucose produces:
explanation: C₆H₁₂O₆ → 2C₂H₅OH + 2CO₂ (fermentation)

subject: Chemistry
text: The functional group −COOH is found in:
explanation: Carboxylic acids contain the −COOH group

subject: Chemistry
text: What is the first member of the alkyne series?
explanation: Ethyne (acetylene, C₂H₂) is the first alkyne — minimum 2 carbons for a triple bond

subject: Chemistry
text: Esterification involves the reaction of an alcohol with:
explanation: Ester + water are formed when alcohol reacts with carboxylic acid

subject: Chemistry
text: The IUPAC name for CH₃−CH₂−OH is:
explanation: CH₃CH₂OH has 2 carbons + OH group = ethanol

subject: Chemistry
text: The molar mass of H₂O is:

subject: Chemistry
text: Avogadro's number is approximately:
explanation: One mole contains 6.02 × 10²³ particles (Avogadro's number)

subject: Chemistry
text: Moles = mass / ?
explanation: n = m/M (moles = mass / molar mass)

subject: Chemistry
text: How many moles are in 44g of CO₂? (M = 44 g/mol)

subject: Chemistry
text: In the reaction 2H₂ + O₂ → 2H₂O, the mole ratio H₂:O₂ is:
explanation: Coefficients give mole ratios: 2 moles H₂ per 1 mole O₂

subject: Chemistry
text: The empirical formula shows the:
explanation: Empirical formula: simplest whole-number ratio of atoms

subject: Chemistry
text: Concentration (mol/dm³) = moles / ?
explanation: c = n/V

subject: Chemistry
text: The molar mass of NaCl is: (Na=23, Cl=35.5)

subject: Chemistry
text: At STP, 1 mole of any gas occupies:
explanation: Molar volume at STP = 22.4 dm³/mol

subject: Chemistry
text: The limiting reagent is the reactant that:
explanation: The limiting reagent is fully consumed and determines how much product forms

subject: Chemistry
text: Elements in the same group have the same number of:
explanation: Group number = number of valence (outer shell) electrons

subject: Chemistry
text: Which group contains the noble gases?
explanation: Noble gases occupy Group 18 (also written Group 0)

subject: Chemistry
text: The period number of an element indicates its number of:
explanation: Period number = number of occupied electron shells

subject: Chemistry
text: Which of these is an alkali metal?
explanation: Alkali metals are Group 1: Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr

subject: Chemistry
text: Electronegativity generally increases as you go:
explanation: Electronegativity increases across a period and decreases down a group

subject: Chemistry
text: The halogens are in:
explanation: Halogens: F, Cl, Br, I, At — Group 17

subject: Chemistry
text: Atomic radius generally decreases across a period because:
options: Nuclear charge increases, pulling electrons closer
Electrons are added to new shells
explanation: More protons pull electrons closer without adding new shells → smaller radius

subject: Chemistry
text: Which property makes metals good electrical conductors?
explanation: Metals have delocalised (free) electrons that carry electric charge

subject: Chemistry
text: Transition metals are found in:
explanation: Transition metals are the d-block elements, Periods 4–7, Groups 3–12

subject: Chemistry
text: An element with electron configuration 2,8,7 is in Group:
explanation: 7 valence electrons → Group 17 (Halogens, e.g. Chlorine)

subject: Chemistry
text: During electrolysis, reduction occurs at the:
explanation: Cathode is negative — attracts cations which gain electrons (reduction)

subject: Chemistry
text: The electrode that is connected to the positive terminal is the:
explanation: Anode = positive electrode; attracts anions

subject: Chemistry
text: What is deposited at the cathode during electrolysis of CuSO₄?
explanation: Cu²⁺ ions are reduced at the cathode: Cu²⁺ + 2e⁻ → Cu

subject: Chemistry
text: Faraday's First Law states that the mass of substance deposited is:
explanation: m ∝ Q (mass deposited ∝ quantity of charge passed)

subject: Chemistry
text: An electrolyte is a substance that:
options: Conducts electricity when dissolved or molten
Conducts electricity only as a solid
explanation: Electrolytes are ionic compounds that conduct when dissolved or molten

subject: Chemistry
text: During electrolysis of dilute H₂SO₄, what is released at the anode?
explanation: OH⁻ ions are discharged at the anode: 4OH⁻ → 2H₂O + O₂ + 4e⁻

subject: Chemistry
text: Electroplating uses electrolysis to:
options: Coat a metal with another metal
explanation: Electroplating deposits a thin metal coating on an object

subject: Chemistry
text: In the electrolysis of molten NaCl, Na is deposited at the:
explanation: Na⁺ + e⁻ → Na at cathode; Cl⁻ → ½Cl₂ + e⁻ at anode

subject: Chemistry
text: What is Q in Faraday's equation Q = It?
explanation: Q = charge (Coulombs) = Current (A) × time (s)

subject: Chemistry
text: The Faraday constant F ≈
explanation: F = 96485 ≈ 96500 C per mole of electrons

subject: Chemistry
text: The pH of a neutral solution is:
explanation: pH 7 is neutral — pure water at 25°C

subject: Chemistry
text: An acid is defined as a proton:
explanation: Brønsted-Lowry: an acid is a proton (H⁺) donor

subject: Chemistry
text: Litmus turns red in:
explanation: Litmus indicator turns red in acidic conditions

subject: Chemistry
text: The product of acid + base neutralisation is:
explanation: Acid + base → salt + water (neutralisation reaction)

subject: Chemistry
text: HCl is an example of a:
explanation: HCl is a strong acid — fully dissociates in water

subject: Chemistry
text: A solution with pH 2 is:
explanation: Low pH (1-3) indicates a strongly acidic solution

subject: Chemistry
text: NaOH is a:
explanation: Sodium hydroxide (NaOH) is a strong base — fully dissociates

subject: Chemistry
text: A buffer solution:
explanation: Buffers maintain approximately constant pH when small amounts of acid/base are added

subject: Chemistry
text: The colour of methyl orange in acid is:
explanation: Methyl orange: red in acid, yellow in base

subject: Chemistry
text: The salt produced when HNO₃ reacts with NaOH is:
explanation: HNO₃ + NaOH → NaNO₃ + H₂O (sodium nitrate)
